column3: Upon doing A, B happens / When A, B
column5: 教室に入ると先生がレオ君に言いました。
column6: Upon entering the classroom, the teacher spoke to Leo-kun.

column5: お母さんが起こしてくれなかったんです。
column6: Because my mother didn't wake me up.
column7: In the negative past, 〜てくれなかった means someone did not do something for me/us.

column3: Command telling someone to do something
column7: A firm but less blunt command form often used by parents or teachers.

column4: Verb (ない-stem) + ければなりません
column5: レオ君は早く寝て、早く起きなければなりません
column6: Leo-kun has to go to bed early and get up early.

column5: レオ君は泣きながら走りました。

column3: They did things like singing songs and doing an animal quiz on the bus
column4: Verb (past tense) + り, Verb (past tense) + りする
column5: バスの中で歌を歌ったり、動物クイズをしたりしました
column6: On the bus, they did things like sing songs and do an animal quiz.

column3: After going to the bathroom, he ended up not knowing the way
column5: トイレに行って道がわからなくなって...
column6: I went to the bathroom and then I couldn't find my way back...
